column1: Agentic AI benefits include better performance, {{c1::parallelization}}, and {{c2::modularity}} (swapping tools/models).
column2: Additional notes: These factors make agentic workflows superior to non-agentic ones for complex reasoning.

column1: {{c1::Reflection}} is the design pattern where an agent critiques its own output or receives feedback from a critic agent.
column2: External feedback (e.g., code execution errors) makes this significantly more powerful.

column1: {{c1::Task Decomposition}} is the process of breaking a complex task into smaller steps handled by models, tools, or retrieval.
column2: This often yields better results than direct generation or one-shot prompting.

column1: When building agentic systems, research indicates that {{c1::Planning with Code}} (writing scripts) often produces more robust results than simple JSON-based plans.
column2: This allows the LLM to capture logic and control flow more effectively.

column1: To mitigate LLM evaluation bias, one should use a clear {{c1::quality rubric}} with specific grading criteria.
column2: This provides a more structured and objective framework for the LLM to follow.

column1: In agentic development, you should build a {{c1::quick prototype}} first to identify which components perform unsatisfactorily before optimizing.
column2: This "Build <-> Analyze" cycle is more efficient than theoretical planning.
